name: ExtensionClaimResponse_AddItem_BodySite
title: R5: bodySite
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite` 0..* `BackboneElement`
*  R4B: `ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`
*  R4: `ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.

element.comment:
Element `ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.

Element `ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite.site` is part of an existing definition because parent element `ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite.site` has a context of ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `ClaimResponse`.
Element `ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite.site` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
For example: Providing a tooth code allows an insurer to identify a provider performing a filling on a tooth that was previously removed.
element.requirements:
Allows insurer to validate specific procedures. Element `ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite.site` is part of an existing definition because parent element `ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite` requires an extension for additional cardinality.

Element `ClaimResponse.addItem.bodySite.site` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
